title: CE Conformity Process
subtitle: Complete Guide for Machine Safety Specialists
description: Based on Machine Directive 2006/42/EC | Integrate into Project Approach
selectSection: Select a section to view content

overview.heading: Overview: Integrate CE Process into Project Approach
overview.mistakesTitle: ❌ Common Mistakes - What NOT to Do
overview.separateRouteQuote: Machine is ready, let the CE-employee do his MD round
overview.separateRoute1: CE employee encounters unacceptable risks → What now?
overview.copyPasteQuote: We have an older version, make it specific
overview.copyPaste2: Do current standards impose stricter requirements?
overview.bestPracticeLead: Integrate the CE-process into the "normal" design process!
overview.bestPracticeDesc: In all steps in the design and realization process of a machine, the relevant "CE-steps" must be integrated.
overview.stepADesc: Machine description, applicable standards, product directives, Annex IV assessment
overview.stepBDesc: Risk assessment & reduction, essential health & safety requirements, harmonized standards
overview.stepCDesc: Instructions manual, EC declaration of conformity, CE marking

research.heading: A: Research in Advance
research.clarificationTitle: 💡 Important Clarification: Safety Components vs Machines
research.clarificationQ: Common Question: Are PILZ safety relays and similar safety components in Annex IV?
research.clarificationL1: Safety relays, safety PLCs, light curtains etc. = Safety components
research.clarificationL2: These components are listed in Annex IV as "Logic units for safety functions"
research.clarificationL3: But when integrated into complete machine, need to assess if entire machine is in Annex IV
research.annexIVWarning: ⚠️ Machine Directive Annex IV - Machines Requiring Special Attention

standards.heading: Standards Classification and Application
standards.introDesc: European machinery safety standards are organized into three tiers, forming a complete safety framework:

typeA.title: Type A Standards (Basic Safety Standards)
typeA.description: Cover basic concepts, design principles and general aspects applicable to all machinery
examples: EN ISO 12100 - Safety of machinery - General principles for design
EN ISO 14121-1 - Safety of machinery - Risk assessment

typeB.title: Type B Standards (Group Safety Standards)
typeB.description: Deal with one safety aspect or one type of safeguard applicable to a wide range of machinery
examples: EN IEC 60204-1 - Electrical equipment of machines

typeC.title: Type C Standards (Machine Safety Standards)
typeC.description: Provide detailed safety requirements for a specific machine or group of machines
typeC.importance: For Annex IV machines, applying Type C standards can avoid the need for Notified Body (NoBo)
examples: EN ISO 10218-2 - Safety requirements for industrial robot systems
EN 692 - Machine tools - Mechanical presses - Safety

standards.hierarchyB: If no Type C standard exists, apply relevant Type B standards
standards.hierarchyA: Type A standards provide basic principles for all machinery
standards.app1Desc: Does a Type C standard exist for your machine?
standards.app2Desc: Applying it provides presumption of conformity
standards.app3Desc: Combine relevant Type A and Type B standards
standards.app4Desc: Applying appropriate Type C standard can avoid NoBo involvement
standards.robotRow1Title: Equipment for mechanical handling of unit loads
standards.robotRow2Title: Industrial robots - Robot systems and integration

items: Presses with stroke > 6 mm and speed > 30 mm/s
Protective devices designed to detect presence of persons

riskAssessment.title: Risk Assessment Module
riskAssessment.subtitle: Complete Guide for Machine Safety Risk Assessment
riskAssessment.overviewDesc: Risk assessment is the core process of machine safety and must be conducted according to EN ISO 12100 standard.
riskAssessment.must3: Document all assessment processes and results

identification.title: 1. Risk Identification
identification.description: Identify hazards in all phases of machine lifecycle

analysis.title: 2. Risk Analysis
analysis.description: Analyze severity and probability of harm for each hazard

evaluation.title: 3. Risk Evaluation
evaluation.description: Evaluate risk level based on risk analysis results
items: Risk level calculation: R = S × P × F × A
Risk level classification: Low, Medium, High, Very High
Need for further risk reduction measures

reduction.title: 4. Risk Reduction
reduction.description: Implement risk reduction measures and verify effectiveness

technicalFile.title: Technical File Management
technicalFile.subtitle: Complete Guide for CE Conformity Technical File
technicalFile.infoDesc: Technical file is the core evidence of CE conformity and must be complete, accurate and traceable.
technicalFile.legal2: Demonstrate compliance with essential health and safety requirements
technicalFile.legal3: Be kept within EU territory for 10 years
technicalFile.legal4: Be available to competent authorities on request

requirements.title: Technical File Requirements
requirements.description: According to Machine Directive 2006/42/EC Annex VII

structure.title: Recommended File Structure
items: 7. Declaration of conformity and CE marking

maintenance.title: File Maintenance Requirements
maintenance.description: Ongoing maintenance and updates of technical file

retention.title: Retention Period Requirements
retention.description: Legal retention period for technical file
items: Retain 10 years after the last machine manufactured
Provide to competent authorities on request

machineSafetyTraining.subtitle: Machine Safety Specialist (CE) - Complete TAB Module
machineSafetyTraining.tab1CoreDesc: Conformité Européenne - Guarantee of free trade within the European Economic Area
machineSafetyTraining.tab2CoreDesc: Ensure products comply with EU legislation and establish a complete technical documentation system
machineSafetyTraining.tab3CoreDesc: Systematic identification, assessment and reduction of machine risks according to EN-ISO 12100
machineSafetyTraining.tab4CoreDesc: Three-step risk reduction method under the Machinery Directive

machineSafetyTraining.tab5CoreDesc: Dual system of product liability and workplace safety responsibility
machineSafetyTraining.overviewCoreDesc: Systematic learning from legal framework to practical application

machineryModification.title: Existing Machinery Modification Guide
machineryModification.subtitle: Modifying Existing Machinery - Substantial Modification & Compliance
machineryModification.coreLead: When modifying existing machinery, the key question is:
machineryModification.coreBold: "Does this modification constitute a Substantial Modification?"
machineryModification.coreDesc: If it is a substantial modification, the modifier becomes the new manufacturer and must re-do CE conformity assessment.
machineryModification.definitionLead: A modification is considered substantial when it:
machineryModification.def1: Changes the original performance, purpose or type, not covered by the original risk assessment
machineryModification.def2: Changes the nature of hazards or increases risk level (refer to relevant EU harmonised legislation)
machineryModification.def3: Places the product back on the market or into service
machineryModification.q1: Does the modification change the machine's performance, purpose or type?
machineryModification.q1ex: e.g. Manual machine converted to CNC
machineryModification.q2: Does the modification introduce new hazards or increase existing risks?
machineryModification.q3: Does the modification change the safety strategy?
machineryModification.q3ex: e.g. Replacing physical guard with light curtain
machineryModification.q4: Does the machine need to be placed back on the market?
machineryModification.summary: Compliance of machinery modification depends on the nature and extent of the modification, not its scale. Even a small change that alters safety characteristics may be substantial. Conversely, large-scale like-for-like replacement may not be. The key is to assess risk impact honestly.

inspection.title: Inspection Module
inspection.subtitle: Machine Safety Inspection and Verification Guide
inspection.infoDesc: Regular inspection is an important means to ensure continuous compliance of machine safety.

preInspection.title: Pre-Inspection Preparation
preInspection.description: Preparation work before conducting machine safety inspection
items: Collect relevant technical documents and standards

inspectionProcess.title: Inspection Process
items: Visual inspection of machine appearance and structure

documentation.title: Inspection Documentation
documentation.description: Documents to be recorded during inspection
